compiled_state.title: check-usage.sh returns VERDICT:2 (paused) when usage is 7%/77% due to 100x display multiplication
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: During sprint execution, check-usage.sh returned VERDICT: 2 (paused) and displayed USAGE_5HR: 700%, USAGE_7DAY: 7700%. Raw cache at ~/.claude/.usage-cache.json showed five_hour_pct: "7" and seven_day_pct: "77" — actual usage 7%/77%, well below throttle thresholds. The script appears to multiply percentage values by 100 before display and before verdict calculation, causing it to falsely report critical usage and halt sub-agent dispatch. Workaround: read raw cache directly to confirm actual usage and proceed as UNLIMITED.
